title: aphanes occidentalis
intro: Aphanes occidentalis, also known as Western aphanes or Western wood-sorrel, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). Traditionally, the leaves and stems of the Aphanes occidentalis plant have been used in folk medicine, boasting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aphanes occidentalis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote overall well-being.
